title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
In Mrs. Haberdasher's sitting room, Inspector Sharp discusses the investigation into Valerie Hobhouse with Hercule Poirot. Sharp explains that Hobhouse utilized Celia Austin’s kleptomania as a cover for a sophisticated smuggling operation involving drugs and gemstones. While Sharp notes a lack of concrete evidence to charge Hobhouse with Celia's murder, Poirot reveals a different truth regarding the death of Patricia Lane.

Poirot asserts that the 6:08 phone call to the police station, which appeared to be from Patricia Lane, was actually made by Valerie Hobhouse from a pharmacy telephone booth to establish an alibi. Poirot identifies Nigel Chapman as the true murderer of Patricia Lane, arguing that Chapman killed her by hitting her from behind. Poirot notes that Chapman, who possessed morphine and exhibited reckless, overconfident behavior, was able to "recognize" the fake call because he was the one who answered it immediately after committing the crime. Poirot believes Chapman attempted a double bluff by using green ink and planting Lennie Bateson's hair in Patricia's fingernails, but views these as the mistakes of a self-centered man.

Following this, Poirot meets with a lawyer, Mr. Endicott, to discuss the recent death of Arthur Stanley. Poirot inquires about the death of Arthur's wife several years prior, which was ruled an accidental barbiturate overdose. Endicott notes that Arthur had provided evidence regarding his wife's confusion. Poirot concludes the meeting by asking Endicott whether Arthur Stanley was the type of man who would kill his wife in order to marry another woman.
